title: High Protein Meal Prep Chicken & Rice Bowls
metaDescription: High protein meal prep chicken and rice bowls with 42g of protein per container — grilled chicken breast, broccoli, and rice, ready for 5 days in 40 minutes.
author: Recipe Team
keywords: meal prep chicken and rice, high protein meal prep, chicken meal prep bowls
ingredients: 2 cups jasmine or basmati rice, uncooked
Salt, pepper, sesame seeds to taste

steps.name: Cook the rice
steps.text: Cook rice according to package instructions while you prep the chicken and broccoli.

steps.name: Marinate the chicken
steps.text: Whisk 2 tbsp olive oil, soy sauce, honey, and garlic in a bowl. Add chicken breasts and let sit for 10 minutes.

steps.name: Cook the chicken
steps.text: Heat a skillet over medium-high heat. Cook chicken 6-7 minutes per side until internal temperature reaches 165°F. Rest 5 minutes, then slice.

steps.name: Roast the broccoli
steps.text: Toss broccoli with remaining olive oil, salt, and pepper. Roast at 425°F for 15 minutes, or sauté in a hot pan for 5-6 minutes.

steps.name: Assemble containers
steps.text: Divide rice, chicken, and broccoli evenly between 5 containers. Sprinkle with sesame seeds. Cool completely before sealing and refrigerating.

tips.question: How long do these meal prep bowls last in the fridge?
tips.answer: Up to 5 days when cooled completely before sealing and stored in an airtight container. Reheat only the portion you're eating.

tips.question: Can I freeze these meal prep bowls?
tips.answer: Yes, up to 3 months. Rice and chicken freeze well; broccoli texture softens slightly on reheat but stays edible.

tips.question: Why does my chicken turn out dry when reheated?
tips.answer: Slicing the chicken before storing (rather than reheating a whole breast) and reheating covered with a splash of water helps retain moisture — dry reheats are usually from uncovered microwaving on high power.

tips.question: Can I swap the rice for something lower carb?
tips.answer: Yes — cauliflower rice or quinoa both work as direct swaps without changing the chicken or broccoli steps.
